message:
test(conformance): differential harnesses for ctype.h and string.h vs glibc

Two new differential conformance harnesses that call BOTH the FrankenLibC
implementation and the host glibc reference on identical inputs, then
assert outputs match. Pattern 1 (Differential Testing) from the
testing-conformance-harnesses skill.

ctype.h (15 tests, 3598 differential calls):
- Sweeps EOF + every byte 0..=255 through 12 classifiers
  (isalpha, isdigit, isalnum, isspace, isupper, islower, isprint,
  ispunct, isxdigit, isblank, iscntrl, isgraph) and 2 transformers
  (toupper, tolower).
- Classifiers compared on truthiness; transformers on exact value.

string.h (10 tests, 9 pure functions):
- strchr, strrchr — char-position (offset-or-null compare)
- strstr — substring search incl. empty needle / overlapping needle
- strpbrk, strspn, strcspn — set-based scans
- strnlen — bounded length incl. usize::MAX
- memchr — bounded byte search incl. NUL byte and zero bound
- memcmp — sign-of-result compare (POSIX-portable)
- 5–13 cases per function covering: empty, single-char, no-match,
  match-at-start/end, embedded NUL, high bytes, overlapping needles,
  bound-cuts-match, bound-includes-match.

Result: 25/25 new tests pass, ZERO divergences against glibc.
ctype.h and string.h pure-function families are conformant.

Coverage stamps emitted as JSON-line records for CI parsing:
  {"family":"ctype.h","reference":"glibc","total_diff_calls":3598,"divergences":0}
  {"family":"string.h","reference":"glibc","functions":9,"divergences":0}

Full abi suite: 3001/3001 pass (was 2976; +25 from new harnesses).

CONFORMANCE: libc ctype.h diff matrix
CONFORMANCE: libc string.h diff matrix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
